title: Ghost
description: When you die but your soul lingers, *refusing to pass through the Black Gates*, you gain this insert, and choose 1 Consequence (see reverse).

submoves.title: Instinct
submoves.description: Lose the Instinct from your playbook, and replace it with one of these (your choice):
pickOne: Denial - To refuse to accept that you are dead.
Obsession - To pursue your Terrible Purpose no matter what.
Ennui - To bemoan your condition, to wish for release.

submoves.title: Unliving
submoves.description: You do not breathe. You need not eat nor drink nor sleep. You do not heal normally. You gain no benefit from magical healing, Make Camp, Recover or Convalesce.

submoves.title: Disembodied
submoves.description:
Your body is dead and gone, but you persist as an incorporeal spirit. You can be harmed only by that which harms spirits or ghosts, and you have the barest influence on the material world.

You normally go unseen, the material world dim and distorted to you. When you manifest a ghostly presence in shadows or darkness, the world becomes clear again. For each of the following you wish to be true, lose 1d4 HP:

bullets: You appear solid and whole, much as you did in life
You can speak clearly and intelligibly
Your touch (or ghostly weapons) can harm the living (ignores armor)

outcomes.text: You remain manifest for as long as you concentrate; pain or shock or direct sunlight threaten your concentration, for sure.

submoves.title: Tethered
submoves.description:
Choose something to which you are bound: your mortal remains, the place where you died, an object of personal significance, etc.

____tether____

When you are reduced to 0 HP, mark a consequence (see reverse) and your essence disperses until the next sunset. You reform near your tether with half your max HP. If your tether has been destroyed, mark the Final Consequence.

title: Revenant
description: When you die but *cling stubbornly to your body, refusing to pass through the Black Gates*, you gain this insert, and choose 1 Consequence (see reverse).

submoves.title: Instinct
submoves.description: Lose the Instinct from your playbook, and replace it with one of these (your choice):
pickOne: Denial - To refuse to accept that you are dead.
Obsession - To pursue your Terrible Purpose no matter what.
Ennui - To bemoan your condition, to wish for release.

submoves.title: Unliving
submoves.description: You do not breathe. You need not eat nor drink nor sleep. You do not heal normally. You gain no benefit from magical healing, Make Camp, Recover or Convalesce.

submoves.title: Undying
submoves.description:
Neither poison nor disease do you harm. You feel little pain. When you take damage from cutting, stabbing, or crushing, take half damage (after armor, rounded up).

When you are reduced to 0 HP, roll +CON:

outcomes.text: Regain half your max HP and choose 1:
Regain half your max HP and choose 2:
Either regain 1 HP and all 3 apply, or give up this insert and gain the Ghost insert instead.

bullets: You're out of the action until the next sunset
Your body is permanently maimed in some way of the GM's choosing

outcomes.text: If your body is completely destroyed (burnt to ash, ground to jelly, etc.), treat it as if you were reduced to 0 HP and rolled a 6-.

submoves.title: Implacable
submoves.description: When you push the limits of your undead body, lose 1d4 HP and choose 1:
pickOne: Perform a feat of inhuman strength
Act with uncanny speed and grace
Refuse to be moved, held back, or knocked off course

title: Thrall
description: When you die but *call on one of the Things Below by name and beseech them to intercede*, they do. Death is averted, but you gain this insert and 1 Mark

submoves.title: Your Master
submoves.description:
The Thing Below that you called upon? The one that plucked your soul from the Black Gates and hides you from the Pale Hunter? It owns you now.Name your master here, along with any titles that you know:

____master______

submoves.title: Instinct
submoves.description: Loose the Instinct from your playbook, and replace it with one of these (your choice):
pickOne: Fascination - To explore your powers, your master, your new existence
Resistance - To struggle against your master and cling to humanity
Shame - To hide and deny your true nature

submoves.title: Favor
submoves.description:
Your favor starts at 0 and can go no higher than 3. When you *have 3 Favor and would gain another, reduce your Favor to 0 and choose 1:

- Ask a question of your master and gain advantage on your next roll to act on the answer

- Gain a new Mark of your choice. Then ask the GM to choose a Mark that you don't have, and cross it off - you can never gain it.

submoves.title: Impulse
submoves.description:
Ask the GM to describe an impulse that represents your master's nature and will  ______impulse______ 

When the GM compels you to act on your impulse, gain 1 Favor if you act as bidden. If you resist roll +WIS:

bullets: Start acting as compelled, but stop yourself before you go too far
Harm yourself (d6 damage, ignores armor) to regain control

outcomes.text: You come to your senses later, having done gods-know-what
When you **act on your impulse without being compelled to do so**, and your actions cause you or your allies trouble, gain 1 Favor

submoves.title: Dark Succor
submoves.description: When you are dying or killed outright, your master intercedes on your behalf. You will recover, here and now or at a time and place of the GM's choosing. Then, roll +Favor:

bullets: Gain a new Mark of the GM's choice
Cross off a Mark that you don't have—you can never gain it
Your master gives you a task; until you complete it, your Favor stays at 0

submoves.title: Unholy Vessel
submoves.description: When you would gain a Mark but there are none left to gain, your humanity is utterly lost. You become a threat in the GM's control. Make a new character.
